raw_consumer_statement: I have been working with Macys XXXX XXXX since I received a statement in XXXX with a {$490.00} fraudulent charge. Ive talked to at least 4, maybe 5 representatives, all with deep foreign accents, I live in Ohio and speak and understand XXXX. Each time I have to repeat my story as if the person before, whom I spoke to for over an hour didnt take notes. My case was recently closed on XX/XX/XXXX with a letter stating I have the merchandise and didnt return it. I have no merchandise, never did, so there is nothing to return. I mailed in quite a bit of information to Macys XXXX XXXX, so supposedly they are opening my case back up. Macys customer service told me back the first of XXXX to not pay the fraudulent charge and that there would be no interest fees attached. Now I just received a statement with interest fees attached. They did say yesterday they would wave the fees. They can not seem to be able to reach this fraudulent company either. I cant with the number that Macys XXXX XXXX provided to me. The name of the company is XXXX. It used to be a legitimate company charging around {$39.00} for a year of computer back up service. Once I no longer had a computer in XXXX of XXXX I sent them an email stating I no longer had a computer so please cancel my service. They did not have a phone number that I could find. They tell you to email them to cancel, which I did. I was never charged a ridiculously high charge of {$490.00} ever. Since Ive found out it is no longer the company I thought, that it is a fraudulent company that takes money and offers no legit service for and cant be cancelled. I expect my credit card to help me when I call them immediately for help, not drag me out for months!
